modules.name: Production Process Master
modules.description: Defines and manages production process types and their configurations to enable structured production tracking and real-time process monitoring in the fenestration industry.

api_endpoints.summary: List and search production processes with pagination and sorting
Get details of a specific production process by ID

error_responses.condition: Process with given ID not found

api_endpoints.summary: Create a new production process

body.name: processCode
body.notes: Unique code identifier, e.g. CUT, CRP

body.name: sequenceNumber
body.notes: Defines execution order, must be unique

body.name: finalProcess
body.validation: only one process can be final

body.name: setupScanning
body.notes: Enables barcode scanning for this process

body.name: barcode
body.notes: Unique barcode for scanning, e.g. CUT-001

validations: Process Code must be unique
Process Name is mandatory
Sequence Number must be unique
Only one Final Process allowed
Total Process Weightage across all active processes must not exceed 100%
If Setup Scanning is enabled, Barcode is required and must be unique

error_responses.condition: Validation errors such as duplicate codes, missing mandatory fields, or invalid weightage

api_endpoints.summary: Update an existing production process

body.name: finalProcess
body.validation: only one process can be final

body.name: barcode
body.validation: required if setupScanning=true, unique except current

validations: Process Code must be unique
Process Name is mandatory
Sequence Number must be unique
Only one Final Process allowed
Total Process Weightage across all active processes must not exceed 100%
If Setup Scanning is enabled, Barcode is required and must be unique

error_responses.condition: Validation errors or process not found
Process with given ID not found

api_endpoints.summary: Deactivate (soft delete) a production process
validations: Only active processes can be deactivated

error_responses.condition: Process with given ID not found

api_endpoints.summary: Export production process list to Excel
Generate or retrieve barcode for a production process

fields.name: barcode
fields.notes: Unique barcode string for the process

error_responses.condition: Process with given ID not found or barcode not set

api_endpoints.notes: Barcode used for scanning and automatic process updates

models.name: ProductionProcess
models.description: Represents a production process step in the manufacturing workflow

fields.name: processCode
fields.notes: Unique code identifier, uppercase alphanumeric with optional dash/underscore

fields.name: sequenceNumber
fields.notes: Defines execution order, must be unique and positive

fields.name: finalProcess
fields.notes: Indicates if this is the final process in the sequence

fields.name: setupScanning
fields.notes: If true, barcode scanning is enabled for this process

fields.name: isProcessOnQuantity
fields.notes: If true, process tracks quantity produced

fields.name: default
fields.notes: Indicates if this process is a system default

fields.name: active
fields.notes: Indicates if the process is active and used in tracking

fields.name: barcode
fields.notes: Unique barcode string used for scanning; required if setupScanning is true

business_logic.rule: Process Code must be unique across all processes
business_logic.trigger: On create or update of a production process

business_logic.rule: Process Name is mandatory and cannot be empty
Sequence Number must be unique and positive
Only one process can be marked as Final Process at any time
Sum of Process Weightage of all active processes must not exceed 100%
If Setup Scanning is enabled, Barcode must be provided and unique
Only active processes are used in job tracking and production flow
Final Process must have the highest sequence number among all processes
Deactivating a process sets active=false but does not delete the record

workflows.name: Production Process Export Workflow
steps: Validate user authorization
Fetch all production processes
Format data into Excel file
Provide file for download
workflows.outcome: User receives Excel export of production processes

workflows.name: Barcode Generation and Scanning Workflow
workflows.trigger: GET /production-processes/{id}/barcode API call and operator scanning event
steps: Generate or retrieve unique barcode for process
Operator scans barcode during production
System updates job status automatically based on scanned process
workflows.outcome: Real-time tracking of job progress via barcode scanning

modules.name: Job Production Tracking
modules.description: Monitors job execution through configurable processes using flexible barcode-based tracking from fabrication to installation, providing real-time job progress, process-wise tracking, and integration with job, material, and delivery modules.

api_endpoints.summary: List and search job production tracking records with filters and pagination

query.name: securedJobDateTo
query.validation: must be >= securedJobDateFrom if both provided

query.name: deliveryDateTo
query.validation: must be >= deliveryDateFrom if both provided

query.name: status
query.notes: One of All, Secured Job, In Production, Pending Dispatch, Dispatched, Installation In Progress, Completed

query.name: sort_by
query.validation: must be one of jobNumber, orderConfirmationDate, deliveryDate, status

fields.name: data
fields.notes: Array of job production tracking records

validations: securedJobDateTo must be greater than or equal to securedJobDateFrom if both provided
deliveryDateTo must be greater than or equal to deliveryDateFrom if both provided

error_responses.condition: Invalid date range or invalid query parameters

api_endpoints.notes: Supports filtering by job number, dates, status, and search text with pagination and sorting

api_endpoints.summary: Get detailed information of a specific job production tracking record

fields.name: indicators
fields.notes: Material indicators like Profile, Glass, Accessories, Hardware with green or blank

fields.name: processProgress
fields.notes: Process progress per process with completed/total quantities and color status

validations: jobNumber must exist and be secured

api_endpoints.summary: Edit and update job production configuration and details

body.name: processConfiguration
body.notes: Array of selected/customized processes per job

validations: Job must be secured to allow editing
Status must be one of defined enum values
Process configuration must be valid and processes must exist in master list

error_responses.condition: Invalid input data or status transition not allowed

api_endpoints.notes: Allows modification of job production configuration including status and process selection

api_endpoints.summary: View job production history records

fields.name: historyRecords
fields.notes: List of historical changes and events for the job

validations: jobNumber must exist

api_endpoints.summary: Export job production tracking data to Excel
validations: Date ranges must be valid if provided

api_endpoints.summary: Generate and save barcodes for a job

body.name: barcodeType
body.notes: One of Window/Position, Cut-wise, Profile Detail

validations: Job must be secured
Barcode type must be valid
Barcode generation must follow barcode structure rules

error_responses.condition: Invalid barcode type or job not secured

api_endpoints.notes: Supports generating mandatory window/position barcode and optional cut-wise or profile detail barcodes

api_endpoints.summary: Scan a barcode to update production tracking status
validations: Job must be secured
Process must be active
Barcode must be generated before scanning
Duplicate barcode scanning not allowed

error_responses.condition: Invalid barcode or process code or barcode not generated

api_endpoints.notes: Updates production tracking status based on scanned barcode and process

api_endpoints.summary: Assign or customize production processes for a job

body.name: processes
body.validation: each process must exist in master process list

validations: Processes must be valid and selectable from master list
Job must be secured
api_endpoints.notes: Allows user to select, deselect, or customize processes per job

models.name: JobProductionTracking
models.description: Represents a production job with tracking details from fabrication to installation

fields.name: indicators
fields.notes: Material indicators like Profile, Glass, Accessories, Hardware with green or blank

fields.name: processProgress
fields.notes: Process progress per process with completed/total quantities and color status

relationships.description: Processes configured and customized per job

models.name: ProcessConfiguration
models.description: Represents a production process configuration for a job

fields.name: selected
fields.notes: Indicates if process is selected for the job

models.name: Barcode
models.description: Represents a barcode generated and used for production tracking

business_logic.rule: Job must be secured before allowing production tracking or barcode scanning
business_logic.trigger: On job status update or barcode scan attempt

business_logic.rule: Process must be active to allow barcode scanning and progress update
Barcode must be generated before scanning; duplicate barcode scans are not allowed
Position barcode is mandatory; cut barcode is optional; system must allow no cut barcode scenario
Job status can only transition through defined statuses in order: Secured Job → In Production → Pending Dispatch → Dispatched → Installation In Progress → Completed
Glazed indicator is automatically set to Yes if Glass exists in job, else No
Delivery status is linked and synchronized with delivery note (DN) module
Deleting or deactivating a job must cascade update related process configurations and barcodes

workflows.name: Barcode Generation Flow
steps: Open job and verify it is secured
Select barcode type (Window/Position, Cut-wise, Profile Detail)
Generate barcode string following defined structure
Save barcode record
Print barcode label
workflows.outcome: Barcode generated, saved, and ready for production tracking
workflows.rollback: If generation or save fails, no barcode is created

workflows.name: Barcode Scanning Flow
steps: Scan barcode and identify job and position
Identify process from input
Validate job is secured and process is active
Update production tracking status for the process and job
Prevent duplicate scans
workflows.outcome: Production tracking status updated based on barcode scan
workflows.rollback: If validation fails, no status update occurs

workflows.name: Job Status Transition Workflow
steps: Validate requested status transition is allowed
Update job status
Trigger cascading updates if needed (e.g., delivery status)
Notify relevant stakeholders if applicable
workflows.outcome: Job status updated following allowed transitions

workflows.name: Export Job Production Tracking Data
steps: Validate filter parameters
Query job production tracking data
Generate Excel file with data
Provide file for download
workflows.outcome: User receives Excel export of job tracking data

modules.name: Production Scanning
modules.description: Handles real-time updates of production progress via barcode scanning or manual entry, integrating job production tracking and batch production tracking with operator accountability and minimal manual effort.

api_endpoints.summary: Create a new production scan record by scanning or entering a barcode

body.name: processId
body.validation: must be a valid process from Production Process Master

body.name: barcode
body.validation: must exist and be linked to a job; no duplicate completion
body.notes: Barcode is mandatory and must be valid

body.name: jobNumber
body.validation: if provided, must match barcode's linked job

body.name: batchNumber
body.validation: if provided, must be a valid batch; else system auto-assigns batch using FIFO and pending quantity logic
body.notes: Optional batch selection; system auto-assigns if omitted

body.name: scanMode
body.validation: must be one of Manual, Reader, Mobile

body.name: scanByUserId
body.validation: must be an identified user; defaults to logged-in user unless overridden by user barcode scan

body.name: department
body.validation: must be one of Production, Dispatch, Site

fields.name: scanId
fields.notes: Unique identifier of the created scan record

fields.name: status
fields.notes: Scan status: Success, Duplicate, or Error

validations: Process is mandatory and must be valid
Barcode must exist and be linked to a job
No duplicate completion allowed
If jobNumber provided, must match barcode's job
User must be identified
Batch must be valid if provided
Process must not be already completed for the scanned position

error_responses.condition: Quantity exceeded for batch or job

api_endpoints.notes: On success, updates job production tracking and batch production tracking in real time.

api_endpoints.summary: List or search production scan records with optional filters

fields.name: data
fields.notes: Array of production scan records with fields: barcode, profileName, unitValue, scanByUser, jobNumber, batchNumber, customerName, processName, productionScanTime, productionScanStatus, productionScanQty, materialCategory, orientation, scanMode, remarks

api_endpoints.notes: Supports filtering by process, job, batch, user, scan mode, and status.

models.name: ProductionScan
models.description: Represents a single production scan record capturing barcode scan details and production progress updates

fields.name: barcode
fields.notes: Scanned barcode linked to job position or material cut

fields.name: profileName
fields.notes: Item or window profile name or position

fields.name: productionScanQty
fields.notes: Quantity scanned; defaults to 1 per scan

fields.name: materialCategory
fields.notes: Material category such as Profile, Glass, etc.

fields.name: orientation
fields.notes: Orientation if applicable: Left, Right, Top, Bottom

relationships.description: Each scan is linked to one production process
Each scan is performed by one user

models.name: ProductionProcessMaster
models.description: Master list of production processes available for scanning

models.name: JobProductionTracking
models.description: Tracks production progress at job level per process

fields.name: status
fields.notes: Current status of the process for the job

models.name: BatchProductionTracking
models.description: Tracks production progress at batch level per process

fields.name: status
fields.notes: Current status of the batch process

models.name: User
models.description: Represents a system user who can perform production scans

business_logic.rule: Every scan must update Job Production Tracking as the primary layer
Every scan must update Batch Production Tracking as the secondary layer
Position barcode scans update full position and job + batch tracking; cut barcode scans update piece and job + batch tracking
If process is quantity based, completed quantity increases and pending quantity recalculated; else only status updated
Process status can only transition through Pending → In Progress → Completed
Batch allocation follows FIFO logic: if batch not selected, system auto-assigns earliest active batch with pending quantity
business_logic.trigger: On scan creation when batch not selected

business_logic.rule: User must be identified before scanning; logged-in user is default, user barcode scan can override
No duplicate completion allowed for the same barcode and process
Barcode must exist and be linked to a job; job filter must match barcode's job if provided
On successful scan save, history is updated and timestamp recorded

workflows.name: Final Production Scanning Workflow
workflows.trigger: User submits a scan via the production scanning screen
steps: Open Production Scanning Screen
User selects process
User optionally selects job
User optionally selects batch
User scans or enters barcode
System validates barcode, process, job, batch, and user
System identifies job and position from barcode
System identifies batch (user selected or auto-assigned)
System updates process status and quantities
System updates Job Production Tracking
System updates Batch Production Tracking
Scan data grid is updated with new scan record
workflows.outcome: Production scan recorded with real-time updates to job and batch tracking; scan history grid reflects latest data
workflows.rollback: If validation fails or update errors occur, scan is rejected with error message; no partial updates applied

modules.name: Batch-wise Production Tracking
modules.description: Groups one or multiple jobs into production batches to enable staged fabrication, dispatch, and installation with real-time tracking and barcode integration, optimizing operations and supporting multi-job batch execution.

api_endpoints.summary: List and search production batches with pagination and sorting
Get details of a specific production batch by Batch No

fields.name: jobs
fields.notes: List of jobs with positions and quantities

api_endpoints.summary: Create a new production batch with selected jobs and positions

body.name: jobs
body.notes: Array of jobs with positions and batch quantities

body.name: positionNo
body.validation: must exist and belong to job

body.name: batchQty
body.notes: Quantity assigned to batch for this position

validations: Batch Qty must not exceed available Qty for each position
Total Batch Qty equals sum of all batch quantities across selected positions
Batch must include at least one job and one position
Batch date must be valid

error_responses.condition: Validation errors such as batchQty exceeding availableQty or missing required fields

api_endpoints.notes: Batch No is auto-generated by the system

api_endpoints.summary: Edit or update an existing production batch

body.name: jobs
body.notes: Updated list of jobs with positions and batch quantities

body.name: positionNo
body.validation: must exist and belong to job

body.name: batchQty
body.notes: Quantity assigned to batch for this position

validations: Batch Qty must not exceed available Qty for each position
Batch must be active to allow updates
Batch date must be valid if updated

api_endpoints.summary: Delete or deactivate a production batch
validations: Batch must be active to delete
Deleting batch must update related job tracking accordingly

error_responses.condition: Batch cannot be deleted due to status or dependencies

api_endpoints.notes: Soft-delete or deactivate batch rather than hard delete

api_endpoints.summary: Update the status of a production batch

body.name: status
body.validation: one of Created,In Production,Production Completed,Dispatched,Partially Installed,Installed

validations: Status transitions must follow allowed flow: Created → In Production → Production Completed → Dispatched → Partially Installed → Installed
Only authorized roles can update status

api_endpoints.summary: Scan barcode to update production, dispatch, or installation status for batch
validations: Barcode must map to Job → Position → Batch
Batch must be active
Quantity updated must not exceed pending quantities for the process
FIFO applied if multiple batches exist for same job and position

error_responses.condition: No active batch found for barcode

api_endpoints.notes: Supports production, dispatch, and installation updates via barcode scanning

api_endpoints.summary: Export batch data to Excel
api_endpoints.notes: Exports fields: Batch No, Job, Position, Qty, Produced, Dispatched, Installed

models.name: Batch
models.description: Represents a production batch grouping one or multiple jobs and their positions for staged tracking

fields.name: batchDate
fields.notes: Date of batch creation or planned production

fields.name: totalJobs
fields.notes: Number of jobs included in batch

fields.name: totalBatchQty
fields.notes: Sum of batch quantities across all positions

relationships.description: Batch contains multiple job positions with quantities

models.name: BatchJobPosition
models.description: Represents the association of a job and position within a batch including assigned quantities and tracking

fields.name: totalQty
fields.notes: Total quantity required for this position

fields.name: availableQty
fields.notes: Remaining quantity available for batch allocation

fields.name: producedQty
fields.notes: Quantity produced for this position in batch

fields.name: dispatchedQty
fields.notes: Quantity dispatched for this position in batch

fields.name: installedQty
fields.notes: Quantity installed for this position in batch

models.name: Job
models.description: Represents a production job that can be included in batches

relationships.description: Job has many positions in batches

business_logic.rule: Batch quantity assigned to a position must not exceed the available quantity for that position.
Total batch quantity is the sum of all batch quantities assigned across selected positions and jobs.
Batch status transitions must follow the sequence: Created → In Production → Production Completed → Dispatched → Partially Installed → Installed.
Dispatch quantity must not exceed produced quantity for the batch.
Installation quantity must not exceed dispatched quantity for the batch.
When a batch is deleted or deactivated, related job tracking quantities must be updated accordingly.
Barcode scanning must correctly map to Job → Position → Batch and update the corresponding batch and job tracking quantities.
business_logic.trigger: Barcode scan for production, dispatch, or installation

business_logic.rule: If multiple active batches exist for the same job and position, FIFO (first created batch first) logic must be applied to allocate scanned quantities.
business_logic.trigger: Barcode scan when multiple batches exist

business_logic.rule: Batch must be active to allow production, dispatch, installation updates or modifications.

workflows.name: Batch Status Lifecycle
workflows.trigger: API call to update batch status
steps: Batch created with status 'Created'
Status updated to 'In Production' when production starts
Status updated to 'Production Completed' when production finishes
Status updated to 'Dispatched' when batch is dispatched
Status updated to 'Partially Installed' when partial installation occurs
Status updated to 'Installed' when installation completes
workflows.outcome: Batch progresses through defined statuses reflecting real production stages
workflows.rollback: Invalid status transitions are rejected and previous status retained

workflows.name: Barcode Scan Allocation
steps: Identify Job and Position from barcode
Find active batch(es) containing the job and position with pending quantity
If multiple batches found, select batch by FIFO (earliest created)
Update batch and job position produced, dispatched, or installed quantities accordingly
workflows.outcome: Accurate real-time update of batch and job tracking based on barcode scans
workflows.rollback: If no active batch found or invalid barcode, return error and do not update

workflows.name: Batch Creation and Allocation
steps: Validate selected jobs and positions with available quantities
Calculate total batch quantity
Create batch record with status 'Created'
Create batch-job-position allocations with assigned quantities
workflows.outcome: New batch created with correct job and position allocations
workflows.rollback: On validation failure, batch creation aborted with error

workflows.name: Batch Deletion/Deactivation
workflows.trigger: API call to delete or deactivate batch
steps: Verify batch is active and deletable
Mark batch as deleted or inactive (soft delete)
Update related job and position tracking quantities to reflect removal
workflows.outcome: Batch is deactivated and related tracking data updated
workflows.rollback: If dependencies prevent deletion, operation aborted with error

modules.name: Material Consumption
modules.description: This module records, tracks, and manages the actual quantity of material consumed by the Production Team after material has been issued against a specific Job. It ensures accurate tracking of issued, consumed, returned, and remaining material quantities for production accountability and audit.

api_endpoints.summary: List and filter material consumption summary records

query.name: jobNumber
query.validation: Must correspond to an existing issued job number

query.name: materialType
query.validation: One of Profile, Hardware, Accessories, Glass, Other

query.name: sort_by
query.validation: Must be a valid sortable field

fields.name: data
fields.notes: Array of material consumption summary objects

fields.name: total_count
fields.notes: Total number of records matching filter

validations: Only active issued material records appear
Job Number filter must correspond to issued jobs

error_responses.condition: Invalid filter parameters or pagination values

api_endpoints.summary: Retrieve consumption history entries for a specific job and material

path.name: jobNumber
path.validation: Must correspond to an existing issued job number

path.name: materialCode
path.validation: Must correspond to a material code issued for the job

query.name: sort_by
query.validation: Must be a valid sortable field

fields.name: data
fields.notes: Array of consumption history entry objects

validations: Job Number and Material Code must correspond to issued material
Only active issued records appear

api_endpoints.summary: Add a new material consumption entry for a specific job and material

path.name: jobNumber
path.validation: Must correspond to an existing issued job number

path.name: materialCode
path.validation: Must correspond to a material code issued for the job

body.name: consumptionDate
body.validation: Must be a valid date, not in the future

body.name: consumedQuantity
body.validation: Must be numeric, greater than zero, and less than or equal to available consumption balance

body.name: consumedBy
body.validation: Must be a valid production user/operator identifier
body.notes: User or operator who consumed the material

body.name: processName
body.notes: Optional production process name (e.g. Cutting, Assembly)

fields.name: consumptionEntryId
fields.notes: Unique identifier of the created consumption entry

validations: Consumed quantity must not exceed available consumption balance
Job Number and Material Code must be from issued material
Remaining quantity cannot be negative

error_responses.condition: Validation failure such as consumed quantity exceeding available balance or missing required fields
Job Number or Material Code not found or not issued

api_endpoints.notes: Confirmation popup recommended on frontend before saving

api_endpoints.summary: Export material consumption summary and history to Excel

query.name: jobNumber
query.validation: Must correspond to an existing issued job number

query.name: materialType
query.validation: One of Profile, Hardware, Accessories, Glass, Other

fields.name: fileUrl
fields.notes: URL to download the generated Excel file

api_endpoints.notes: Export includes summary grid and optional consumption history sheet; format: Excel

api_endpoints.summary: Generate printable report of material consumption

query.name: jobNumber
query.validation: Must correspond to an existing issued job number

fields.name: printUrl
fields.notes: URL to access the printable report

validations: Job Number must correspond to issued material
api_endpoints.notes: Print output includes job info, material summary, quantities, and detailed consumption history

models.name: MaterialConsumptionSummary
models.description: Summary of material consumption for a specific job and material line

fields.name: jobNumber
fields.notes: Job against which material was issued

fields.name: materialUser
fields.notes: User to whom material was originally issued

relationships.description: Consumption entries linked to this summary

models.name: MaterialConsumptionEntry
models.description: Individual consumption entry recording quantity consumed on a specific date and process

fields.name: consumedQuantity
fields.notes: Quantity consumed in this entry, must be > 0

fields.name: consumedBy
fields.notes: User or operator who consumed the material

relationships.description: Belongs to a material consumption summary

business_logic.rule: Material Consumption can only be recorded for material already issued against a job; no fresh stock issue allowed
business_logic.trigger: On adding or updating consumption entries

business_logic.rule: Consumed Quantity must be numeric, greater than zero, and cannot exceed available consumption balance (Issued Quantity - Consumed Quantity - Returned Quantity)
Returned Quantity is automatically linked from the Material Issued Return module and cannot be manually edited
business_logic.trigger: On loading or updating summary data

business_logic.rule: Remaining Quantity is always system-calculated as Issued Quantity minus Consumed Quantity minus Returned Quantity and cannot be negative
business_logic.trigger: On saving consumption entries or updating returns

business_logic.rule: Consumption history must be maintained for audit and tracking purposes
Only active issued material records appear in the module
business_logic.trigger: On querying summary or consumption entries

workflows.name: Material Consumption Recording Workflow
workflows.trigger: User opens Material Consumption module and selects a Job Number
steps: Load issued material summary grid filtered by selected Job Number
User clicks 'Add Consumption' on a material row
Display consumption entry form with auto-populated fields and calculated available consumption
User enters consumption details and confirms save
System validates input, saves consumption entry, updates summary consumed and remaining quantities
Refresh summary grid and consumption history grid
workflows.outcome: Accurate recording of material consumption with updated summary and history
workflows.rollback: If validation fails or user cancels, no changes are saved

workflows.name: Material Consumption Export Workflow
steps: System gathers filtered summary and consumption history data
Generate Excel file with summary and optional history sheets
Provide download link to user
workflows.outcome: User obtains Excel report for review and audit

workflows.name: Material Consumption Print Workflow
workflows.trigger: User requests print output for a job
steps: System compiles job details, material summary, quantities, and consumption history
Generate printable report
Provide access to printable report
workflows.outcome: User obtains printable report for internal tracking

merged_artifacts.reason: DB model 'job_production_tracking' appears in multiple modules (job_production_tracking, production_scanning) with compatible schemas. Merged 18 field(s) from all definitions.

issues.description: Graph node 'material_consumption' has no edges (no dependencies defined).

nodes.label: Production Process Master
nodes.description: Defines and configures the sequence of production processes for job execution with scanning-based tracking and job-wise flow control.

nodes.label: Job Production Tracking
nodes.description: Monitors and controls the progress and status of production jobs using barcode-based tracking and process-wise monitoring.

nodes.label: Production Scanning
nodes.description: Captures and records production progress in real time by scanning or manually entering barcodes linked to job positions or material cuts.

nodes.label: Batch-wise Production Tracking
nodes.description: Manages and tracks production batches grouping multiple jobs for staged fabrication, dispatch, and installation with barcode integration.

nodes.label: Material Consumption
nodes.description: Records and manages actual quantity of material consumed by production after material issuance against specific jobs.

edges.description: Job Production Tracking uses master process definitions from Production Process Master to configure and customize production processes per job.
Production Scanning requires updating job production progress and validating job references linked to scanned barcodes in Job Production Tracking.
Production Scanning requires updating batch production quantities and validating batch references linked to scanned barcodes in Batch-wise Production Tracking.
Production Scanning obtains the list of valid production processes from Production Process Master for selection in the scanning workflow.
